title: Add ≥1 more language to ≥100 reviewed terms
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual's M4 goal is to scale languages and terms while maintaining quality and tracking outcomes. Following first delivery (delivery-001), this task expands coverage to at least 1 additional under-served language with at least 100 reviewed terms, each following the full two-stage review pipeline (clinical + advocate sign-off on source, then qualified translation review + back-translation QA for safety-load terms).
objective: Expand the glossary to cover at least 1 additional under-served language with at least 100 reviewed and translation-reviewed terms, following the established two-stage review pipeline and license gates.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 1 additional language (qualifying per the PLAN target-language selection rule) has at least 100 translation-reviewed and signed-off terms

Each term follows the full pipeline: source definition with clinical + advocate sign-off, then translation with qualified translation-reviewer sign-off and back-translation QA for terms with safetyLoad other than none
A qualified medical-translation reviewer for the new language pair is confirmed (COI on file) before translation begins
license-002 passes for every new entry (attribution, provenance, notMedicalAdvice:true, license-compatible)
Outcomes are tracked per the outcomes-001 log (adoption status, defects, readability, usefulness)
output: glossary/<new-lang>/<term-id>.yaml for at least 100 reviewed terms in the new language, with translation review sign-offs and QA results; delivered package for the partner
